word: G4874 - συναναμίγνυμι - synanamígnymi - soon-an-am-ig'-noo-mee - Have company with

sections.paragraph:
- Original: συναναμίγνυμι

- Transliteration: sunanamignumi

- Phonetic: soon-an-am-ig'-noo-mee

- Definition:

1. to mix up together

2. to keep company with, be intimate with one

- Origin: from G4862 and a compound of G303 and G3396

- TDNT entry: 21:12,1

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G4862 and a compound of G303 and G3396; to mix up together that is (figuratively) associate with: - (have keep) company (with).
